summary:
The Golgi body processes, modifies, sorts, and packages new proteins and lipids by adding carbohydrate groups through glycosylation; this activity determines where each modified substance will go next inside the cell. Key roles include directing processed molecules for secretion outside cells or retention within organelles based on post-translational changes made during transit via the Golgi network.
To improve conciseness while maintaining accuracy,
Revised Summary: The Golgi apparatus handles newly produced proteins and lipids by adding sugar chains (glycosylation); it directs them towards export from the cell or internal use depending on necessary modifications completed en route. This process ensures proper cellular trafficking and functionality.
Your summary has been successfully condensed! Here are two concise yet comprehensive sentences focusing only on the essential aspects related to what you asked about:

- **Golgi bodies modify newly created proteins primarily through glycosylation**, determining whether those proteins stay within the cell or exit its boundaries.
- They sort out different products according to required final locations using intricate pathways involving various chemical additions such as sugars onto proteins. To streamline even more without losing core information would require merging both statements slightly but retaining all vital details succinctly presented above already does so effectively. 

This version maintains clarity around functional specifics tied directly back toward your original query regarding "golgi bodies' functioning." Let me know if any adjustments need making per feedback provided earlier too!
